title: Goroutine Pools, Context Causes, Backpressure, and Shutdown · Execution Trace: Graceful Join
summary: A goroutine is launched per URL, workers close shared channels, cancellation stops fetch but not parsing or writes, and main returns before cleanup. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go graceful join increment in bounded leak-free Go crawl pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Stop admission, cancel permitted operations, close by owner, await goroutines, flush durable state, close transports, and prove no leak.
Collect per-item outcomes without abandoning siblings, leaking goroutines, hiding first cause, or converting policy stops into errors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Competency probe: Stop admission, cancel permitted operations, close by owner, await goroutines, flush durable state, close transports, and prove no leak.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Graceful Join model · Theory Graceful Join, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Exiting main cleans goroutines and network resources gracefully.
